title: Manhunt

Can't get past gate in Born Again
title: Manhunt

notes:
At first I encountered a bug which prevented me from passing a gate in the tutorial area. This is a known bug which also impacts many Windows users. The problem went away after I replaced manhunt.exe with a patched executable found here: https://steamcommunity.com/app/12130/discussions/0/828939978361803059/
The game now runs flawlessly.
title: Manhunt

notes: Wont launch in fullscreen. Game itself runs flawlessly.
title: Manhunt

notes: It wouldn't work full screen at first until I rebooted. I also had a controller plugged in, so the game ignored the mouse inputs for some reason. Once I unplugged the controller and rebooted, it worked a treat.
title: Manhunt

notes:
I added no tweaks whatsoever to Manhunt.

The game runs well and at a silky 60fps roughly but the hair pulling part is that the game crashes 1 minute after an action or if an action isn't preformed in time.

The gates in Born Again, the first level you start on, have an even bigger chance of being permanently shut if you don't go through them in time. You'll be forced to run through and take on the hunter that's about to go through the gate, which drains a lot of your health.

Some controls need to be mapped twice in order for them to work. Do this before play.

So far there isn't a way to get past the fact the gates become permanently shut in every game session and the fact that the game crashes on occasions yet but I'm certain that Proton might get a fix for this.
title: Manhunt

notes:
Update on the performance and other bugs for Manhunt.

I mentioned in my last report that Manhunt had the potential of crashing when you preformed certain actions 10 seconds to a minute after preforming them.

I also forgot to add that picking up items would cause the game to freeze and crash.

However, installing this patch for Manhunt (assuming you run the Steam version: https://community.pcgamingwiki.com/files/file/540-manhunt-steam-fix/) removes all of this and makes the game fully operational. 

Just be aware that your controls hold the potential of locking up and some controls not responding. I suggest remapping all of your controls to the controls already in use or to what you want. It's not required but it can be helpful in preventing certain controls locking up.
